milestone: Only-one-content-type Experiences — card foot + Working View kind-gating fix
slice: Two real bugs found right after Build 0048's only-one-content-kind change went in, both reported directly during authoring. (1) The content card's own foot duplicated the Ownership section's own "Make Public" action -- same graduateToPublic call in two places, and it silently showed nothing at all for a still-Nurturing Experience. The card foot is now purely informational (Hosted By / Usage / Lifecycle), never an action -- the Ownership section (Graduate to Personal, Graduate to Public, Make Public, the permanent no-reverse-path messaging) remains the one and only place lifecycle actions live, completely unchanged and unaffected by this fix. (2) Switching the Content Type tabs updated Runtime Preview correctly but not Working View. Root cause: the isolated Experience Studio (_renderExperienceStudio) painted every populated section simultaneously with no awareness of exp.contentKind at all -- since switching kinds is deliberately non-destructive (an inactive section's own data survives a switch, for later), an already-populated Text/Image/Graphics/Colour section kept rendering in Working View regardless of which tab was active, while the real Engine Adapter (ProjectModel._syncUniversalContent) was already correctly gated by kind, so the two views had silently drifted apart. Fixed by gating _renderExperienceStudio's four paint blocks by kind, and by making _universalContentFootprint (used both for the Adapter's own Colour backdrop rect and for Working View's isolated-canvas crop bounds) kind-aware instead of computing a stale bounding box across every section regardless of which was active.
